results.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a next-generation full-stack monorepo starter. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a Next.js app-router/RSC web app, shared tRPC API, edge-ready database layer using Kysely plus Prisma schema management, PlanetScale-style database status, Clerk-style auth, shadcn-style UI blocks, Expo mobile workspace notes, and a monorepo health panel.
warnings: timed out waiting for sandbox files to match the requested app

dom.title: One App, Five Stacks
dom.text:
IMPLEMENTATION COMPARISON EXPLORER

One App, Five Stacks

A comparison explorer for the same product built in Ruby on Rails, Elixir Phoenix, Go plus GraphQL plus React SPA, classic Next.js/T3 stack, and Next.js app-router/RSC.

RUBY ON RAILS
Fast CRUD
ELIXIR PHOENIX
Realtime
GRAPHQL
Go API + SPA
T3 / RSC
Typed React
RUBY ON RAILS
Convention lane

Rails wins when server-rendered CRUD speed and integrated defaults matter most.

ELIXIR PHOENIX
Realtime lane

Phoenix makes live updates and fault tolerance a first-class tradeoff.

GO + GRAPHQL + REACT SPA
Service split lane

Go plus GraphQL plus React SPA separates backend performance from frontend iteration.

T3
Classic Next.js/T3 stack

T3 emphasizes type-safe full-stack React with familiar deployment ergonomics.

RSC
Next.js app-router/RSC

App Router and RSC shift more work to the server component boundary.

LINE OF CODE
Tradeoff matrix

Line of code comparison, mock deployment links, and tradeoff matrix make the differences visible.

Action loop
Open tradeoff matrix
Compare line of code
View deployment links

results.prompt: Create a complete TypeScript library monorepo starter, not a marketing page. It must include package metadata, workspace setup, a core library entrypoint, strict tsconfig, a build script, tests, changeset/release notes, and a tiny Prisma generator example that shows how generated enums or types can reduce bundle size.

results.prompt: Create a runnable TypeScript CLI tool that scaffolds good project defaults. It should use an interactive prompt, let users select ESLint plus Prettier, VS Code settings and recommended extensions, strict TSConfig, and GitHub Actions CI with pnpm caching. Include the bin entry, CLI source, template writers, and an example generated workflow.
